text.title: decimal-as-string-pattern Decision Tree
text.summary: Field-by-field routing: when to use int, float, pattern-string, or free-string.

decision-tree.root-question: Does this field's exact textual form matter for downstream correctness (billing, identity match, format dispatch)?

branch.question: Is the value numeric and lossy drift tolerable (sensor noise, ML scores, counts)?

branch.conclusion: Use JSON `integer`. Counts/ranks/indices do not need string + pattern.
Use JSON `number` (float). Acceptable for measurements where a few ULPs of drift do not matter.
Use JSON `string` WITHOUT a pattern. Free-form text needs no decode-time constraint.

branch.question: Does the field match a known format in the pattern catalog (money, semver, ISO-8601, IBAN, big-int, E.164)?

branch.conclusion: Use JSON `string` with the catalog pattern + a `description` naming the format and example. Parse to native type on consume.
Author a domain-specific pattern that is the strictest expression of the legitimate distribution. Pair with description + example + the one edge case that has bitten before.

text.example: q-exact-form → branch(true) → q-catalog-match → branch(true) → conclusion(r2-strictest-pattern). Result: `^\\d+\\.\\d{2}$` + description "USD price as decimal string. Example: '19.99'.".
